scenario: Catalyst Marketplace runs Camunda 8.8 with 9500 orders per day. After launching a batch move of 700 stuck order instances onto a corrected payment service task, the on-call operator must track progress and confirm exactly how many of the 700 process instances have been modified before signing off the maintenance window.
question: Where does the operator monitor batch modification progress and check how many process instances have already been modified?

options.text: On a dedicated Migrations dashboard linked from the cluster home page, which polls the broker for batch progress counters and refreshes every minute.
In the Decisions tab, where each modified instance lists per-element timing data alongside a batch operation status counter that ticks down to zero.
In the BPMN viewer overlay, which animates each moved instance until the batch operation finishes and the live counter rolls back automatically to zero.
In the operations panel on the instances list view, by clicking the Instances link in the entry to see how many process instances have been modified.

a.text: Incorrect. There is no separate Migrations dashboard for batch modification monitoring; the docs direct operators to the operations panel on the instances list view after the batch starts.

b.text: Incorrect. The Decisions tab is not where batch modification progress is tracked; the documented monitoring surface is the operations panel on the process instance list view.

c.text: Incorrect. The BPMN viewer does not animate per-instance batch progress; monitoring is done through the operations panel entry on the instances list view.

d.text: Correct. The docs state that after the batch modification is started the user is directed to the instances list view, where progress can be tracked in the operations panel and the Instances link on the entry shows how many process instances have been modified.

explanation: After starting a batch modification, Operate directs the user to the instances list view, where progress is tracked through the operations panel; clicking the Instances link on the corresponding operations entry reveals how many process instances have been modified so far.
